Natural habitat: Humid tropical forest; often epiphytic or semi-epiphytic on trees and rocky slopes in warm, sheltered conditions.
Temperature / hardiness target: Warm tropical conditions; ideal 18–28°C; avoid cold drafts and temperatures below about 15°C.
Light needs: Bright indirect light; no harsh midday sun under glass.
Humidity: High humidity preferred, ideally 60–80% with good air movement.
Water needs: Keep evenly moist in growth, but never waterlogged; reduce slightly in winter while avoiding full drought.
Soil / substrate: Airy tropical/epiphyte-style substrate with excellent drainage and moisture retention.
Recommended substrate mix (%): 35% fine orchid bark; 25% coco coir/peat-free compost; 20% perlite/pumice; 10% leaf mould/compost; 10% charcoal/coarse organic matter.
Winter hardiness in Heiligenhaus: Not hardy outdoors; tropical greenhouse or warm bright indoor conditions only.
Rarity in cultivation: Uncommon but available in specialist tropical/houseplant trade
Bonsai potential: Low; better as a flowering greenhouse specimen than a bonsai subject.
Invasive potential: Low in Germany; not winter-hardy and unsuitable for outdoor escape.
Conservation / endangerment level: Not evaluated / no global threat flag recorded in this catalogue.
Pollinators / ecology: Tropical forest ornamental with showy pendant inflorescences; flowers and berries are part of bird/insect ecology in native habitat, exact pollinators not central for cultivation.
Edible / medicinal / ethnobotanical: Primarily ornamental; no edible use recorded for this catalogue.
Seed source / propagation: Usually propagated by cuttings or specialist nursery production; seed possible but uncommon in hobby cultivation.
Where to buy EU/UK: Araflora (NL); PLNTS.com (NL); Ben’s Jungle (DE); specialist tropical houseplant shops; availability seasonal.
Notes: Spectacular tropical greenhouse plant with glossy leaves and large hanging pink inflorescences. Good future greenhouse specimen; not ideal for unstable/travel conditions. Added from shop sighting; catalogue image should use Wikimedia/Wikidata reference images, not the shop photo.
